title: claim record — records/claims/CLM-NNNN.json
description: One external claim, one owned file (C23/C17) — exploded from the V1 docs/CLAIMS.json monolith by `gen migrate-claims` (M4c; the legacy monolith’s checker read retired at the M7 contraction). Field semantics are V1's, adversarially earned: statement + type + build_state + blast_radius always; existential claims need a de_risk_milestone; novelty/competitive/market claims need prior_art_checked + how_we_differ (those conditionals are enforced by the CLAIM rules, which activate at descriptor maturity 'claimed' — this schema binds shape, not applicability).

id.description: The record's identity — must match the filename.

slug.description: The V1 register id (e.g. 'wedge-migration'), preserved by gen migrate-claims so cross-references survive the explosion.

statement.description: The claim itself, as asserted to the outside world.

type.description: Claim species — drives which CLAIM rules apply.

build_state.description: How real the claim is right now.

blast_radius.description: What it costs if the claim is wrong.

de_risk_milestone.description: For existential claims: the concrete step that converts assumption into evidence.

prior_art_checked.description: When the prior-art sweep was last done (novelty/competitive/market claims; CLAIM-04 staleness reads this).

confidence.description: Honest confidence in the claim as stated.

citations.description: Evidence: each citation must say what it supports and why (CLAIM-06).

supports_because.description: Why this source supports the claim — a bare link is not evidence.
